words.definitionEn: the ability to control your thoughts and actions in order to achieve what you want to do; a strong and determined desire to do something that you want to do
words.exampleEn: to have a strong will

words.definitionEn: a victory in a game, contest, etc.
words.exampleEn: two wins and three defeats

words.definitionEn: one of the parts of the body of a bird, insect or bat that it uses for flying
words.exampleEn: The swan flapped its wings noisily.

words.definitionEn: to get on a ship, train, plane, bus, etc.
words.exampleEn: Passengers are waiting to board.

words.definitionEn: before a particular period of time has passed; during a particular period of time
words.exampleEn: You should receive a reply within seven days.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of surprise and pleasure that you have when you see or experience something beautiful, unusual or unexpected
words.exampleEn: He retained a childlike sense of wonder.

words.definitionEn: to think about something and try to decide what is true, what will happen, what you should do, etc.
words.exampleEn: ‘Why do you want to know?’ ‘No particular reason. I was just wondering.’

words.definitionEn: the soft hair that covers the body of sheep and some other animals
words.exampleEn: Sheep were kept for their wool and meat.

words.definitionEn: affecting all parts of the world
words.exampleEn: an increase in worldwide sales

words.definitionEn: affecting all parts of the world
words.exampleEn: an increase in worldwide sales

words.definitionEn: the state of worrying about something
words.exampleEn: The threat of losing their jobs is a constant source of worry to them.

words.definitionEn: less well
words.exampleEn: Working-class children fared rather worse.

words.definitionEn: most badly or seriously
words.exampleEn: He was voted the worst dressed celebrity.

words.definitionEn: having a value in money, etc.
words.exampleEn: Our house is worth about £100 000.

words.definitionEn: expressed in writing rather than in speech
words.exampleEn: Having a written record of what I've done is very valuable.

words.definitionEn: in a way that produces a result that is not correct or that you do not want
words.exampleEn: My name is spelt wrong.

words.definitionEn: a weapon designed to explode at a particular time or when it is dropped or thrown
words.exampleEn: a bomb goes off/explodes

words.definitionEn: a piece of land next to or around your house where you can grow flowers, fruit, vegetables, etc., usually with a lawn (= an area of grass)
words.exampleEn: They have a gorgeous old oak tree in their front yard.

words.definitionEn: to attack something/somebody by leaving a bomb in a place or by dropping bombs from a plane
words.exampleEn: Warplanes bombed targets in and around the capital.

words.definitionEn: young people considered as a group
words.exampleEn: It's a movie that will appeal to the young.

words.definitionEn: the time of life when a person is young, especially the time before a child becomes an adult
words.exampleEn: in somebody's youth, He had been a talented musician in his youth.

words.definitionEn: the line that divides two countries or areas; the land near this line
words.exampleEn: I live in a small town in the US, near the Canadian border.

words.definitionEn: to spend time and/or energy doing something
words.exampleEn: ‘Shall I wait?’ ‘No, don't bother’.

words.definitionEn: a part of a tree that grows out from the main stem and on which leaves, flowers and fruit grow
words.exampleEn: She climbed the tree and hid in the branches.

words.definitionEn: a type of product, service, etc. made or offered by a particular company under a particular name
words.exampleEn: a well-known brand of toothpaste

words.definitionEn: to apply a brand name, image or identity to something
words.exampleEn: brand something, The website was not doing a very good job of branding the company.

words.definitionEn: willing to do things that are difficult, dangerous or painful; not afraid
words.exampleEn: brave men and women

words.definitionEn: the air that you take into your lungs and send out again
words.exampleEn: His breath smelt of garlic.

words.definitionEn: to take air into your lungs and send it out again through your nose or mouth
words.exampleEn: He breathed deeply before speaking again.

words.definitionEn: the action of taking air into the lungs and sending it out again
words.exampleEn: Her breathing became steady and she fell asleep.

words.definitionEn: a woman on her wedding day, or just before or just after it
words.exampleEn: a toast to the bride and groom

words.definitionEn: a particular thing that somebody does
words.exampleEn: You have committed a serious criminal act.

words.definitionEn: a ball of air or gas in a liquid, or a ball of air inside a solid substance such as glass
words.exampleEn: champagne bubbles

words.definitionEn: to place a dead body in the ground
words.exampleEn: bury somebody/something, They killed her and buried her body.

words.definitionEn: past
words.exampleEn: Just drive by. Don't stop.

words.definitionEn: not excited, nervous or upset
words.exampleEn: It is important to keep calm in an emergency.

words.definitionEn: a quiet and peaceful time or situation
words.exampleEn: The police appealed for calm.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something become quiet and more relaxed, especially after strong emotion or excitement
words.exampleEn: Have some tea; it'll calm your nerves.

words.definitionEn: a series of planned activities that are intended to achieve a particular social, commercial or political aim
words.exampleEn: an anti-smoking campaign

words.definitionEn: to take part in or lead a campaign, for example to achieve social or political change, or in order to win an election
words.exampleEn: The party campaigned vigorously in the north of the country.

words.definitionEn: the buildings of a university or college and the land around them
words.exampleEn: university/college campuses

words.definitionEn: a person who is trying to be elected or is applying for a job
words.exampleEn: a presidential candidate

words.definitionEn: a type of soft flat hat with a peak (= a hard curved part sticking out in front). Caps are worn especially by men and boys, often as part of a uniform
words.exampleEn: to wear a cap.

words.definitionEn: the person in charge of a ship or commercial aircraft
words.exampleEn: Captain Cook

words.definitionEn: not giving enough attention and thought to what you are doing, so that you make mistakes
words.exampleEn: careless driving

words.definitionEn: a group of people or things with particular features in common
words.exampleEn: These are the nominees from each category.

words.definitionEn: the top inside surface of a room
words.exampleEn: a large room with a high ceiling

words.definitionEn: a special event that people organize in order to celebrate something
words.exampleEn: The occasion was the 40th anniversary celebrations of the orchestra.

words.definitionEn: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service
words.exampleEn: The TV ads were first run last year.

words.definitionEn: in the centre of an area or object
words.exampleEn: central London

words.definitionEn: to be the person or thing around which most activity takes place; to make somebody/something the central person or thing
words.exampleEn: centre around/round somebody/something, State occasions always centred around the king.

words.definitionEn: a public or religious occasion that includes a series of formal or traditional actions
words.exampleEn: More than 1 000 people attended the ceremony.

words.definitionEn: a series of connected metal rings, used for pulling or fastening things; a length of chain used for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: She wore a heavy gold chain around her neck.

words.definitionEn: a new or difficult task that tests somebody’s ability and skill
words.exampleEn: a tough/major/significant challenge

words.definitionEn: a person, team, etc. that has won a competition, especially in a sport
words.exampleEn: the world/European/national/Olympic champion

words.definitionEn: a television station
words.exampleEn: a television/TV channel

words.definitionEn: a separate section of a book, usually with a number or title
words.exampleEn: to read/write a chapter

words.definitionEn: the amount of money that somebody asks for goods and services
words.exampleEn: admission charges

words.definitionEn: to ask an amount of money for goods or a service
words.exampleEn: The fees charged by some companies are excessive.

words.definitionEn: for a low price
words.exampleEn: I got this dress cheap in a sale.

words.definitionEn: a person who cheats, especially in a game
words.exampleEn: You little cheat!

words.definitionEn: to trick somebody or make them believe something that is not true
words.exampleEn: cheat somebody/something, She is accused of attempting to cheat the taxman.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is added to something else
words.exampleEn: All of these technologies are fairly recent additions.

words.definitionEn: happy, and showing it by the way that you behave
words.exampleEn: You're not your usual cheerful self today.

words.definitionEn: connected with chemistry
words.exampleEn: changes in the chemical composition of the atmosphere

words.definitionEn: a substance obtained by or used in a chemical process
words.exampleEn: toxic chemicals

words.definitionEn: the top part of the front of the body, between the neck and the stomach
words.exampleEn: chest pains

words.definitionEn: the period of somebody’s life when they are a child
words.exampleEn: childhood, adolescence, and adulthood

words.definitionEn: a statement that something is true although it has not been proved and other people may not agree with or believe it
words.exampleEn: claim about somebody/something, The company had made false claims about its products.

words.definitionEn: to say that something is true although it has not been proved and other people may not believe it
words.exampleEn: claim (that)…, He claims (that) he was not given a fair hearing.

words.definitionEn: a group of words that includes a subject and a verb, and forms a sentence or part of a sentence
words.exampleEn: in a clause, There are languages that require the subject to come before the object in a clause.

words.definitionEn: to remove things that are not wanted or needed from something
words.exampleEn: clear something, The settlers cleared the land and planted crops.

words.definitionEn: the act of pressing the button on a computer mouse or touchpad
words.exampleEn: a mouse click

words.definitionEn: to choose a particular function or item on a computer screen, etc., by pressing one of the buttons on a mouse or touchpad
words.exampleEn: Click here to add your opinion to the survey.

words.definitionEn: a person who uses the services or advice of a professional person or organization
words.exampleEn: She's a well-known lawyer with many famous clients.

words.definitionEn: an act of climbing up a mountain, rock or large number of steps; a period of time spent climbing
words.exampleEn: an exhausting climb

words.definitionEn: near; not far away
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., They sat close together.

words.definitionEn: material made by weaving or knitting cotton, wool, silk, etc.
words.exampleEn: woollen/cotton/linen cloth

words.definitionEn: an object, a piece of evidence or some information that helps the police solve a crime
words.exampleEn: The burglar left no clues.

words.definitionEn: to train somebody to play a sport, to do a job better, or to improve a skill
words.exampleEn: coach somebody, He has coached the team for five years.

words.definitionEn: a hard black mineral that is found below the ground and burnt to produce heat
words.exampleEn: I put more coal on the fire.

words.definitionEn: a small flat piece of metal used as money
words.exampleEn: gold coins

words.definitionEn: a group of objects, often of the same sort, that have been collected
words.exampleEn: He wanted to share his vast art collection with the world.

words.definitionEn: having a particular colour or different colours
words.exampleEn: brightly coloured balloons

words.definitionEn: to come together to form a single thing or group; to join two or more things or groups together to form a single one
words.exampleEn: combine to do something, Hydrogen and oxygen combine to form water.

words.definitionEn: to express an opinion about something
words.exampleEn: He refused to comment until after the trial.

words.definitionEn: connected with the buying and selling of goods and services
words.exampleEn: residential and commercial properties

words.definitionEn: an advertisement on television, on the radio or on a website
words.exampleEn: a TV/television commercial

words.definitionEn: to do something wrong or illegal
words.exampleEn: to commit a crime/an offence

words.definitionEn: to respect somebody for what they have done or to respect their qualities
words.exampleEn: admire somebody/something, I really admire your enthusiasm.

words.definitionEn: the activity or process of expressing ideas and feelings or of giving people information
words.exampleEn: communication between A and B, Good communication between team leaders and members is essential.

words.definitionEn: the process of comparing two or more people or things
words.exampleEn: For Durkheim, comparison was the most important method of analysis in sociology.
